{{nihongo|'''Nagahatabe Shrine'''|長幡部神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

[[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 長幡部神社 |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 天羽槌雄命 |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 丹生様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=長幡部神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''長幡部神社'''（ながはたべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜の{{jalink|神流川 (利根川水系)|Q11590491|神流川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|天羽槌雄神|Q10940642|天羽槌雄命}}。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|賀美郡|Q11634927}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}内「長幡部神社」、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 由緒 === 創祀年代は不詳。神社の由緒では高度な機織り技術を持った渡来系氏族が、神流川南岸の字宮の西的場西に機織りの神「天羽槌雄命」を守護神として祀ったことが起源とされている第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『長幡部神社 御由緒』より 。「長幡」とは絹織物の一種「絁」（あしぎぬ）、社名の「長幡部」はそれを織る技術者集団「長幡部氏」を示す。なお、「長幡部氏」の出自について、『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』では{{jalink|本巣国造|Q11520234}}（{{jalink|三野前国造|Q11358204}}）の祖・{{jalink|神大根王|Q119380336}}とされ、「麻殖氏系譜并家領記」では天羽槌雄命が祖神とされる。日本古来の長幡部氏とは別に、{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月に戦乱を避けるために朝鮮半島の{{jalink|帯方郡|Q704994}}から{{jalink|阿知使主|Q11657596|阿智王}}と共に渡来してきた「{{jalink|七姓漢人|Q17211949}}」『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月条のうち皀（こう）姓の氏族も存在する'''『新撰姓氏録』第23巻 坂上氏条逸文''' ::''誉田天皇諡応神の御世、本国の乱を避けて、母並びに妻子、母弟・遷興徳、七姓の漢人等を率ゐて帰化す。七姓は第一段古記、段光公字畠等、'' ::''一に云ふ員姓是、高向村主、高向史、高向調使、評首、民使主首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に李姓。是、刑部史の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀郭姓。是、坂合部首、佐大首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に朱姓。是、小市佐、秦、宜等の祖なり。'' ::''次に多姓。是、檜前調使等の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀姓。是、大和国宇太郡佐波多村主長幡部等の祖なり。'' ::''次に高姓。是、檜前村主の祖なり。'' 。 {{jalink|天永|Q1193915}}元年（1110年）に神流川の氾濫により社地が流されたため、字宮の西的場から現在の大字長浜に遷座した。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}10年（1582年）6月には、武蔵国賀美郡が{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の家臣の{{jalink|滝川一益|Q953624}}と{{jalink|北条氏政|Q736948}}ら北条軍の「{{jalink|神流川の戦い|Q1058786}}」で戦場となった。兵火により社殿や古文書が焼失した。 江戸時代には、丹生神を土神・水神・火神・金神・木神に見立てたことから、「五所宮」と呼ばれていた。『新編武蔵風土記稿』長浜下郷村 明治5年には、社名を「長幡五社宮」から現在の「長幡部神社」へ復称した。 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|拝殿 '''社格''' * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}） - 村社 * 大正4年（1915年）3月12日 - 神饌幣畠料供進社 === 境内社 === 明治より前に合祀された。 * 久保 稲荷神社第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『皇大神社 御由緒』より 明治41年に合祀した。「皇大神社」は昭和21年（1946年）に住民の強い要望から旧地の久保へ戻された。 * 久保 皇大神社 （祭神）大日孁貴命 * 字宮 丹生社 * 字宮 稲荷社 : 字宮 丹生社の末社 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷社 === 行事 === * 春祭り（3月19日） * 秋祭り（10月19日） === 交通 === * JR{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|神保原駅|Q845133}}よりバス停「西原町」から上里町バスこむぎっち号（南部ルート）「ウニクス」行に乗車。終点「南五明公会堂」下車徒歩15分。 * JR{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|本庄駅|Q844906}}から朝日バス「神泉総合支所」行に乗車。「三町」下車徒歩30分 * JR{{jalink|八高線|Q1372071}}{{jalink|丹荘駅|Q845044}}から朝日バス「本庄駅南口」行に乗車。「三町」下車徒歩30分 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] (利根川水系)|Q11590491|神流川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|天羽槌雄神|Q10940642|天羽槌雄命}}。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|賀美郡|Q11634927}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}内「長幡部神社」、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 長幡部神社 |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 天羽槌雄命 |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 丹生様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=長幡部神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''長幡部神社'''（ながはたべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜の{{jalink|神流川 (利根川水系)|Q11590491|神流川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|天羽槌雄神|Q10940642|天羽槌雄命}}。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|賀美郡|Q11634927}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}内「長幡部神社」、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 由緒 === 創祀年代は不詳。神社の由緒では高度な機織り技術を持った渡来系氏族が、神流川南岸の字宮の西的場西に機織りの神「天羽槌雄命」を守護神として祀ったことが起源とされている第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『長幡部神社 御由緒』より 。「長幡」とは絹織物の一種「絁」（あしぎぬ）、社名の「長幡部」はそれを織る技術者集団「長幡部氏」を示す。なお、「長幡部氏」の出自について、『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』では{{jalink|本巣国造|Q11520234}}（{{jalink|三野前国造|Q11358204}}）の祖・{{jalink|神大根王|Q119380336}}とされ、「麻殖氏系譜并家領記」では天羽槌雄命が祖神とされる。日本古来の長幡部氏とは別に、{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月に戦乱を避けるために朝鮮半島の{{jalink|帯方郡|Q704994}}から{{jalink|阿知使主|Q11657596|阿智王}}と共に渡来してきた「{{jalink|七姓漢人|Q17211949}}」『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月条のうち皀（こう）姓の氏族も存在する'''『新撰姓氏録』第23巻 坂上氏条逸文''' ::''誉田天皇諡応神の御世、本国の乱を避けて、母並びに妻子、母弟・遷興徳、七姓の漢人等を率ゐて帰化す。七姓は第一段古記、段光公字畠等、'' ::''一に云ふ員姓是、高向村主、高向史、高向調使、評首、民使主首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に李姓。是、刑部史の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀郭姓。是、坂合部首、佐大首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に朱姓。是、小市佐、秦、宜等の祖なり。'' ::''次に多姓。是、檜前調使等の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀姓。是、大和国宇太郡佐波多村主長幡部等の祖なり。'' ::''次に高姓。是、檜前村主の祖なり。'' 。 {{jalink|天永|Q1193915}}元年（1110年）に神流川の氾濫により社地が流されたため、字宮の西的場から現在の大字長浜に遷座した。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}10年（1582年）6月には、武蔵国賀美郡が{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の家臣の{{jalink|滝川一益|Q953624}}と{{jalink|北条氏政|Q736948}}ら北条軍の「{{jalink|神流川の戦い|Q1058786}}」で戦場となった。兵火により社殿や古文書が焼失した。 江戸時代には、丹生神を土神・水神・火神・金神・木神に見立てたことから、「五所宮」と呼ばれていた。『新編武蔵風土記稿』長浜下郷村 明治5年には、社名を「長幡五社宮」から現在の「長幡部神社」へ復称した。 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|拝殿 '''社格''' * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}） - 村社 * 大正4年（1915年）3月12日 - 神饌幣畠料供進社 === 境内社 === 明治より前に合祀された。 * 久保 稲荷神社第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『皇大神社 御由緒』より 明治41年に合祀した。「皇大神社」は昭和21年（1946年）に住民の強い要望から旧地の久保へ戻された。 * 久保 皇大神社 （祭神）大日孁貴命 * 字宮 丹生社 * 字宮 稲荷社 : 字宮 丹生社の末社 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷社 === 行事 === * 春祭り（3月19日） * 秋祭り（10月19日） === 交通 === * JR{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|神保原駅|Q845133}}よりバス停「西原町」から上里町バスこむぎっち号（南部ルート）「ウニクス」行に乗車。終点「南五明公会堂」下車徒歩15分。 * JR{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|本庄駅|Q844906}}から朝日バス「神泉総合支所」行に乗車。「三町」下車徒歩30分 * JR{{jalink|八高線|Q1372071}}{{jalink|丹荘駅|Q845044}}から朝日バス「本庄駅南口」行に乗車。「三町」下車徒歩30分 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 長幡部神社 |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 天羽槌雄命 |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 丹生様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=長幡部神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''長幡部神社'''（ながはたべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜の{{jalink|神流川 (利根川水系)|Q11590491|神流川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|天羽槌雄神|Q10940642|天羽槌雄命}}。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|賀美郡|Q11634927}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}内「長幡部神社」、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 由緒 === 創祀年代は不詳。神社の由緒では高度な機織り技術を持った渡来系氏族が、神流川南岸の字宮の西的場西に機織りの神「天羽槌雄命」を守護神として祀ったことが起源とされている第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『長幡部神社 御由緒』より 。「長幡」とは絹織物の一種「絁」（あしぎぬ）、社名の「長幡部」はそれを織る技術者集団「長幡部氏」を示す。なお、「長幡部氏」の出自について、『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』では{{jalink|本巣国造|Q11520234}}（{{jalink|三野前国造|Q11358204}}）の祖・{{jalink|神大根王|Q119380336}}とされ、「麻殖氏系譜并家領記」では天羽槌雄命が祖神とされる。日本古来の長幡部氏とは別に、{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月に戦乱を避けるために朝鮮半島の{{jalink|帯方郡|Q704994}}から{{jalink|阿知使主|Q11657596|阿智王}}と共に渡来してきた「{{jalink|七姓漢人|Q17211949}}」『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月条のうち皀（こう）姓の氏族も存在する'''『新撰姓氏録』第23巻 坂上氏条逸文''' ::''誉田天皇諡応神の御世、本国の乱を避けて、母並びに妻子、母弟・遷興徳、七姓の漢人等を率ゐて帰化す。七姓は第一段古記、段光公字畠等、'' ::''一に云ふ員姓是、高向村主、高向史、高向調使、評首、民使主首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に李姓。是、刑部史の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀郭姓。是、坂合部首、佐大首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に朱姓。是、小市佐、秦、宜等の祖なり。'' ::''次に多姓。是、檜前調使等の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀姓。是、大和国宇太郡佐波多村主長幡部等の祖なり。'' ::''次に高姓。是、檜前村主の祖なり。'' 。 {{jalink|天永|Q1193915}}元年（1110年）に神流川の氾濫により社地が流されたため、字宮の西的場から現在の大字長浜に遷座した。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}10年（1582年）6月には、武蔵国賀美郡が{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の家臣の{{jalink|滝川一益|Q953624}}と{{jalink|北条氏政|Q736948}}ら北条軍の「{{jalink|神流川の戦い|Q1058786}}」で戦場となった。兵火により社殿や古文書が焼失した。 江戸時代には、丹生神を土神・水神・火神・金神・木神に見立てたことから、「五所宮」と呼ばれていた。『新編武蔵風土記稿』長浜下郷村 明治5年には、社名を「長幡五社宮」から現在の「長幡部神社」へ復称した。 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|拝殿 '''社格''' * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}） - 村社 * 大正4年（1915年）3月12日 - 神饌幣畠料供進社 === 境内社 === 明治より前に合祀された。 * 久保 稲荷神社第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『皇大神社 御由緒』より 明治41年に合祀した。「皇大神社」は昭和21年（1946年）に住民の強い要望から旧地の久保へ戻された。 * 久保 皇大神社 （祭神）大日孁貴命 * 字宮 丹生社 * 字宮 稲荷社 : 字宮 丹生社の末社 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷社 === 行事 === * 春祭り（3月19日） * 秋祭り（10月19日） === 交通 === * JR{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|神保原駅|Q845133}}よりバス停「西原町」から上里町バスこむぎっち号（南部ルート）「ウニクス」行に乗車。終点「南五明公会堂」下車徒歩15分。 * JR{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|本庄駅|Q844906}}から朝日バス「神泉総合支所」行に乗車。「三町」下車徒歩30分 * JR{{jalink|八高線|Q1372071}}{{jalink|丹荘駅|Q845044}}から朝日バス「本庄駅南口」行に乗車。「三町」下車徒歩30分 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] '''長幡部神社'''（ながはたべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜の{{jalink|神流川 (利根川水系)|Q11590491|神流川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|天羽槌雄神|Q10940642|天羽槌雄命}}。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|賀美郡|Q11634927}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}内「長幡部神社」、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 由緒 === 創祀年代は不詳。神社の由緒では高度な機織り技術を持った渡来系氏族が、神流川南岸の字宮の西的場西に機織りの神「天羽槌雄命」を守護神として祀ったことが起源とされている第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『長幡部神社 御由緒』より 。「長幡」とは絹織物の一種「絁」（あしぎぬ）、社名の「長幡部」はそれを織る技術者集団「長幡部氏」を示す。なお、「長幡部氏」の出自について、『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』では{{jalink|本巣国造|Q11520234}}（{{jalink|三野前国造|Q11358204}}）の祖・{{jalink|神大根王|Q119380336}}とされ、「麻殖氏系譜并家領記」では天羽槌雄命が祖神とされる。日本古来の長幡部氏とは別に、{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月に戦乱を避けるために朝鮮半島の{{jalink|帯方郡|Q704994}}から{{jalink|阿知使主|Q11657596|阿智王}}と共に渡来してきた「{{jalink|七姓漢人|Q17211949}}」『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月条のうち皀（こう）姓の氏族も存在する'''『新撰姓氏録』第23巻 坂上氏条逸文''' ::''誉田天皇諡応神の御世、本国の乱を避けて、母並びに妻子、母弟・遷興徳、七姓の漢人等を率ゐて帰化す。七姓は第一段古記、段光公字畠等、'' ::''一に云ふ員姓是、高向村主、高向史、高向調使、評首、民使主首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に李姓。是、刑部史の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀郭姓。是、坂合部首、佐大首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に朱姓。是、小市佐、秦、宜等の祖なり。'' ::''次に多姓。是、檜前調使等の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀姓。是、大和国宇太郡佐波多村主長幡部等の祖なり。'' ::''次に高姓。是、檜前村主の祖なり。'' 。 {{jalink|天永|Q1193915}}元年（1110年）に神流川の氾濫により社地が流されたため、字宮の西的場から現在の大字長浜に遷座した。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}10年（1582年）6月には、武蔵国賀美郡が{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の家臣の{{jalink|滝川一益|Q953624}}と{{jalink|北条氏政|Q736948}}ら北条軍の「{{jalink|神流川の戦い|Q1058786}}」で戦場となった。兵火により社殿や古文書が焼失した。 江戸時代には、丹生神を土神・水神・火神・金神・木神に見立てたことから、「五所宮」と呼ばれていた。『新編武蔵風土記稿』長浜下郷村 明治5年には、社名を「長幡五社宮」から現在の「長幡部神社」へ復称した。 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|拝殿 '''社格''' * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}） - 村社 * 大正4年（1915年）3月12日 - 神饌幣畠料供進社 === 境内社 === 明治より前に合祀された。 * 久保 稲荷神社第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『皇大神社 御由緒』より 明治41年に合祀した。「皇大神社」は昭和21年（1946年）に住民の強い要望から旧地の久保へ戻された。 * 久保 皇大神社 （祭神）大日孁貴命 * 字宮 丹生社 * 字宮 稲荷社 : 字宮 丹生社の末社 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷社 === 行事 === * 春祭り（3月19日） * 秋祭り（10月19日） === 交通 === * JR{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|神保原駅|Q845133}}よりバス停「西原町」から上里町バスこむぎっち号（南部ルート）「ウニクス」行に乗車。終点「南五明公会堂」下車徒歩15分。 * JR{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|本庄駅|Q844906}}から朝日バス「神泉総合支所」行に乗車。「三町」下車徒歩30分 * JR{{jalink|八高線|Q1372071}}{{jalink|丹荘駅|Q845044}}から朝日バス「本庄駅南口」行に乗車。「三町」下車徒歩30分 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 長幡部神社 |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 天羽槌雄命 |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 丹生様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=長幡部神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''長幡部神社'''（ながはたべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜の{{jalink|神流川 (利根川水系)|Q11590491|神流川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|天羽槌雄神|Q10940642|天羽槌雄命}}。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|賀美郡|Q11634927}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}内「長幡部神社」、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 由緒 === 創祀年代は不詳。神社の由緒では高度な機織り技術を持った渡来系氏族が、神流川南岸の字宮の西的場西に機織りの神「天羽槌雄命」を守護神として祀ったことが起源とされている第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『長幡部神社 御由緒』より 。「長幡」とは絹織物の一種「絁」（あしぎぬ）、社名の「長幡部」はそれを織る技術者集団「長幡部氏」を示す。なお、「長幡部氏」の出自について、『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』では{{jalink|本巣国造|Q11520234}}（{{jalink|三野前国造|Q11358204}}）の祖・{{jalink|神大根王|Q119380336}}とされ、「麻殖氏系譜并家領記」では天羽槌雄命が祖神とされる。日本古来の長幡部氏とは別に、{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月に戦乱を避けるために朝鮮半島の{{jalink|帯方郡|Q704994}}から{{jalink|阿知使主|Q11657596|阿智王}}と共に渡来してきた「{{jalink|七姓漢人|Q17211949}}」『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月条のうち皀（こう）姓の氏族も存在する'''『新撰姓氏録』第23巻 坂上氏条逸文''' ::''誉田天皇諡応神の御世、本国の乱を避けて、母並びに妻子、母弟・遷興徳、七姓の漢人等を率ゐて帰化す。七姓は第一段古記、段光公字畠等、'' ::''一に云ふ員姓是、高向村主、高向史、高向調使、評首、民使主首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に李姓。是、刑部史の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀郭姓。是、坂合部首、佐大首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に朱姓。是、小市佐、秦、宜等の祖なり。'' ::''次に多姓。是、檜前調使等の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀姓。是、大和国宇太郡佐波多村主長幡部等の祖なり。'' ::''次に高姓。是、檜前村主の祖なり。'' 。 {{jalink|天永|Q1193915}}元年（1110年）に神流川の氾濫により社地が流されたため、字宮の西的場から現在の大字長浜に遷座した。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}10年（1582年）6月には、武蔵国賀美郡が{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の家臣の{{jalink|滝川一益|Q953624}}と{{jalink|北条氏政|Q736948}}ら北条軍の「{{jalink|神流川の戦い|Q1058786}}」で戦場となった。兵火により社殿や古文書が焼失した。 江戸時代には、丹生神を土神・水神・火神・金神・木神に見立てたことから、「五所宮」と呼ばれていた。『新編武蔵風土記稿』長浜下郷村 明治5年には、社名を「長幡五社宮」から現在の「長幡部神社」へ復称した。 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|拝殿 '''社格''' * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}） - 村社 * 大正4年（1915年）3月12日 - 神饌幣畠料供進社 === 境内社 === 明治より前に合祀された。 * 久保 [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 長幡部神社 |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 天羽槌雄命 |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 丹生様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=長幡部神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''長幡部神社'''（ながはたべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜の{{jalink|神流川 (利根川水系)|Q11590491|神流川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|天羽槌雄神|Q10940642|天羽槌雄命}}。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|賀美郡|Q11634927}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}内「長幡部神社」、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 由緒 === 創祀年代は不詳。神社の由緒では高度な機織り技術を持った渡来系氏族が、神流川南岸の字宮の西的場西に機織りの神「天羽槌雄命」を守護神として祀ったことが起源とされている第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『長幡部神社 御由緒』より 。「長幡」とは絹織物の一種「絁」（あしぎぬ）、社名の「長幡部」はそれを織る技術者集団「長幡部氏」を示す。なお、「長幡部氏」の出自について、『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』では{{jalink|本巣国造|Q11520234}}（{{jalink|三野前国造|Q11358204}}）の祖・{{jalink|神大根王|Q119380336}}とされ、「麻殖氏系譜并家領記」では天羽槌雄命が祖神とされる。日本古来の長幡部氏とは別に、{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月に戦乱を避けるために朝鮮半島の{{jalink|帯方郡|Q704994}}から{{jalink|阿知使主|Q11657596|阿智王}}と共に渡来してきた「{{jalink|七姓漢人|Q17211949}}」『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月条のうち皀（こう）姓の氏族も存在する'''『新撰姓氏録』第23巻 坂上氏条逸文''' ::''誉田天皇諡応神の御世、本国の乱を避けて、母並びに妻子、母弟・遷興徳、七姓の漢人等を率ゐて帰化す。七姓は第一段古記、段光公字畠等、'' ::''一に云ふ員姓是、高向村主、高向史、高向調使、評首、民使主首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に李姓。是、刑部史の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀郭姓。是、坂合部首、佐大首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に朱姓。是、小市佐、秦、宜等の祖なり。'' ::''次に多姓。是、檜前調使等の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀姓。是、大和国宇太郡佐波多村主長幡部等の祖なり。'' ::''次に高姓。是、檜前村主の祖なり。'' 。 {{jalink|天永|Q1193915}}元年（1110年）に神流川の氾濫により社地が流されたため、字宮の西的場から現在の大字長浜に遷座した。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}10年（1582年）6月には、武蔵国賀美郡が{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の家臣の{{jalink|滝川一益|Q953624}}と{{jalink|北条氏政|Q736948}}ら北条軍の「{{jalink|神流川の戦い|Q1058786}}」で戦場となった。兵火により社殿や古文書が焼失した。 江戸時代には、丹生神を土神・水神・火神・金神・木神に見立てたことから、「五所宮」と呼ばれていた。『新編武蔵風土記稿』長浜下郷村 明治5年には、社名を「長幡五社宮」から現在の「長幡部神社」へ復称した。 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|拝殿 '''社格''' * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}） - 村社 * 大正4年（1915年）3月12日 - 神饌幣畠料供進社 === 境内社 === 明治より前に合祀された。 * 久保 稲荷神社第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『皇大神社 御由緒』より 明治41年に合祀した。「皇大神社」は昭和21年（1946年）に住民の強い要望から旧地の久保へ戻された。 * 久保 皇大神社 （祭神）大日孁貴命 * 字宮 丹生社 * 字宮 稲荷社 : 字宮 丹生社の末社 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷社 === 行事 === * 春祭り（3月19日） * 秋祭り（10月19日） === 交通 === * JR{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|神保原駅|Q845133}}よりバス停「西原町」から上里町バスこむぎっち号（南部ルート）「ウニクス」行に乗車。終点「南五明公会堂」下車徒歩15分。 * JR{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|本庄駅|Q844906}}から朝日バス「神泉総合支所」行に乗車。「三町」下車徒歩30分 * JR{{jalink|八高線|Q1372071}}{{jalink|丹荘駅|Q845044}}から朝日バス「本庄駅南口」行に乗車。「三町」下車徒歩30分 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 長幡部神社 |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 天羽槌雄命 |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 丹生様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=長幡部神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''長幡部神社'''（ながはたべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜の{{jalink|神流川 (利根川水系)|Q11590491|神流川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|天羽槌雄神|Q10940642|天羽槌雄命}}。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|賀美郡|Q11634927}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}内「長幡部神社」、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 由緒 === 創祀年代は不詳。神社の由緒では高度な機織り技術を持った渡来系氏族が、神流川南岸の字宮の西的場西に機織りの神「天羽槌雄命」を守護神として祀ったことが起源とされている第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『長幡部神社 御由緒』より 。「長幡」とは絹織物の一種「絁」（あしぎぬ）、社名の「長幡部」はそれを織る技術者集団「長幡部氏」を示す。なお、「長幡部氏」の出自について、『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』では{{jalink|本巣国造|Q11520234}}（{{jalink|三野前国造|Q11358204}}）の祖・{{jalink|神大根王|Q119380336}}とされ、「麻殖氏系譜并家領記」では天羽槌雄命が祖神とされる。日本古来の長幡部氏とは別に、{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月に戦乱を避けるために朝鮮半島の{{jalink|帯方郡|Q704994}}から{{jalink|阿知使主|Q11657596|阿智王}}と共に渡来してきた「{{jalink|七姓漢人|Q17211949}}」『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月条のうち皀（こう）姓の氏族も存在する'''『新撰姓氏録』第23巻 坂上氏条逸文''' ::''誉田天皇諡応神の御世、本国の乱を避けて、母並びに妻子、母弟・遷興徳、七姓の漢人等を率ゐて帰化す。七姓は第一段古記、段光公字畠等、'' ::''一に云ふ員姓是、高向村主、高向史、高向調使、評首、民使主首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に李姓。是、刑部史の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀郭姓。是、坂合部首、佐大首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に朱姓。是、小市佐、秦、宜等の祖なり。'' ::''次に多姓。是、檜前調使等の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀姓。是、大和国宇太郡佐波多村主長幡部等の祖なり。'' ::''次に高姓。是、檜前村主の祖なり。'' 。 {{jalink|天永|Q1193915}}元年（1110年）に神流川の氾濫により社地が流されたため、字宮の西的場から現在の大字長浜に遷座した。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}10年（1582年）6月には、武蔵国賀美郡が{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の家臣の{{jalink|滝川一益|Q953624}}と{{jalink|北条氏政|Q736948}}ら北条軍の「{{jalink|神流川の戦い|Q1058786}}」で戦場となった。兵火により社殿や古文書が焼失した。 江戸時代には、丹生神を土神・水神・火神・金神・木神に見立てたことから、「五所宮」と呼ばれていた。『新編武蔵風土記稿』長浜下郷村 明治5年には、社名を「長幡五社宮」から現在の「長幡部神社」へ復称した。 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|拝殿 '''社格''' * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}） - 村社 * 大正4年（1915年）3月12日 - 神饌幣畠料供進社 === 境内社 === 明治より前に合祀された。 * 久保 稲荷神社第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『皇大神社 御由緒』より 明治41年に合祀した。「皇大神社」は昭和21年（1946年）に住民の強い要望から旧地の久保へ戻された。 * 久保 皇大神社 （祭神）大日孁貴命 * 字宮 丹生社 * 字宮 稲荷社 : 字宮 丹生社の末社 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷社 === 行事 === * 春祭り（3月19日） * 秋祭り（10月19日） === 交通 === * JR{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|神保原駅|Q845133}}よりバス停「西原町」から上里町バスこむぎっち号（南部ルート）「ウニクス」行に乗車。終点「南五明公会堂」下車徒歩15分。 * JR{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|本庄駅|Q844906}}から朝日バス「神泉総合支所」行に乗車。「三町」下車徒歩30分 * JR{{jalink|八高線|Q1372071}}{{jalink|丹荘駅|Q845044}}から朝日バス「本庄駅南口」行に乗車。「三町」下車徒歩30分 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}

{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}10年（1582年）6月には、武蔵国賀美郡が{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の家臣の{{jalink|滝川一益|Q953624}}と{{jalink|北条氏政|Q736948}}ら北条軍の「{{jalink|神流川の戦い|Q1058786}}」で戦場となった。兵火により社殿や古文書が焼失した。 江戸時代には、丹生神を土神・水神・火神・金神・木神に見立てたことから、「五所宮」と呼ばれていた。『新編武蔵風土記稿』長浜下郷村 明治5年には、社名を「長幡五社宮」から現在の「長幡部神社」へ復称した。 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|拝殿 '''社格''' * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}） - 村社 * 大正4年（1915年）3月12日 - 神饌幣畠料供進社 === 境内社 === 明治より前に合祀された。 * 久保 稲荷神社第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『皇大神社 御由緒』より 明治41年に合祀した。「皇大神社」は昭和21年（1946年）に住民の強い要望から旧地の久保へ戻された。 * 久保 皇大神社 （祭神）大日孁貴命 * 字宮 丹生社 * 字宮 稲荷社 : 字宮 丹生社の末社 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷社 === 行事 === * 春祭り（3月19日） * 秋祭り（10月19日） === 交通 === * 拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 天羽槌雄命 |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 丹生様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=長幡部神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''長幡部神社'''（ながはたべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|上里町|Q1345287}}長浜の{{jalink|神流川 (利根川水系)|Q11590491|神流川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|天羽槌雄神|Q10940642|天羽槌雄命}}。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|賀美郡|Q11634927}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}内「長幡部神社」、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 由緒 === 創祀年代は不詳。神社の由緒では高度な機織り技術を持った渡来系氏族が、神流川南岸の字宮の西的場西に機織りの神「天羽槌雄命」を守護神として祀ったことが起源とされている第62回伊勢神宮式年遷宮記念『長幡部神社 御由緒』より 。「長幡」とは絹織物の一種「絁」（あしぎぬ）、社名の「長幡部」はそれを織る技術者集団「長幡部氏」を示す。なお、「長幡部氏」の出自について、『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』では{{jalink|本巣国造|Q11520234}}（{{jalink|三野前国造|Q11358204}}）の祖・{{jalink|神大根王|Q119380336}}とされ、「麻殖氏系譜并家領記」では天羽槌雄命が祖神とされる。日本古来の長幡部氏とは別に、{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月に戦乱を避けるために朝鮮半島の{{jalink|帯方郡|Q704994}}から{{jalink|阿知使主|Q11657596|阿智王}}と共に渡来してきた「{{jalink|七姓漢人|Q17211949}}」『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}4年（785年）6月条のうち皀（こう）姓の氏族も存在する'''『新撰姓氏録』第23巻 坂上氏条逸文''' ::''誉田天皇諡応神の御世、本国の乱を避けて、母並びに妻子、母弟・遷興徳、七姓の漢人等を率ゐて帰化す。七姓は第一段古記、段光公字畠等、'' ::''一に云ふ員姓是、高向村主、高向史、高向調使、評首、民使主首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に李姓。是、刑部史の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀郭姓。是、坂合部首、佐大首等の祖なり。'' ::''次に朱姓。是、小市佐、秦、宜等の祖なり。'' ::''次に多姓。是、檜前調使等の祖なり。'' ::''次に皀姓。是、大和国宇太郡佐波多村主長幡部等の祖なり。'' ::''次に高姓。是、檜前村主の祖なり。'' 。 {{jalink|天永|Q1193915}}元年（1110年）に神流川の氾濫により社地が流されたため、字宮の西的場から現在の大字長浜に遷座した。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}10年（1582年）6月には、武蔵国賀美郡が{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}の家臣の{{jalink|滝川一益|Q953624}}と{{jalink|北条氏政|Q736948}}ら北条軍の「{{jalink|神流川の戦い|Q1058786}}」で戦場となった。兵火により社殿や古文書が焼失した。 江戸時代には、丹生神を土神・水神・火神・金神・木神に見立てたことから、「五所宮」と呼ばれていた。『新編武蔵風土記稿』長浜下郷村 明治5年には、社名を「長幡五社宮」から現在の「長幡部神社」へ復称した。 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|拝殿 '''社格''' * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}） - 村社 * 大正4年（1915年）3月12日 - 神饌幣畠料供進社 === 境内社 === 明治より前に合祀された。 * 久保 [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}{{jalink|Kamisato|Q1345287}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{jalink|Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{jalink|Kamisato|Q1345287}}, {{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. Its deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} of {{jalink|Kami District|Q11634927}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}} says it descends from {{jalink|Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336}}, ancestor of {{jalink|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234}} ({{jalink|Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{jalink|Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949}}" who came from {{jalink|Daifang Commandery|Q704994}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{jalink|Achi no Omi|Q11657596|King Achi}} in June of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}'', entry for June of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{jalink|Ten'ei|Q1193915}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{jalink|Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786}}" between {{jalink|Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624}}, retainer of {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{jalink|Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}{{jalink|Jinbohara Station|Q845133}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}{{jalink|Honjō Station|Q844906}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{jalink|Hachikō Line|Q1372071}}{{jalink|Tanshō Station|Q845044}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}

[[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}{{jalink|Kamisato|Q1345287}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{jalink|Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{jalink|Kamisato|Q1345287}}, {{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. Its deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} of {{jalink|Kami District|Q11634927}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}} says it descends from {{jalink|Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336}}, ancestor of {{jalink|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234}} ({{jalink|Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{jalink|Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949}}" who came from {{jalink|Daifang Commandery|Q704994}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{jalink|Achi no Omi|Q11657596|King Achi}} in June of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}'', entry for June of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{jalink|Ten'ei|Q1193915}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{jalink|Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786}}" between {{jalink|Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624}}, retainer of {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{jalink|Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}{{jalink|Jinbohara Station|Q845133}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}{{jalink|Honjō Station|Q844906}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{jalink|Hachikō Line|Q1372071}}{{jalink|Tanshō Station|Q845044}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}{{jalink|Jinbohara Station|Q845133}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}{{jalink|Honjō Station|Q844906}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{jalink|Hachikō Line|Q1372071}}{{jalink|Tanshō Station|Q845044}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}

[[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}{{jalink|Kamisato|Q1345287}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{jalink|Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{jalink|Kamisato|Q1345287}}, {{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. Its deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} of {{jalink|Kami District|Q11634927}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}} says it descends from {{jalink|Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336}}, ancestor of {{jalink|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234}} ({{jalink|Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{jalink|Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949}}" who came from {{jalink|Daifang Commandery|Q704994}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{jalink|Achi no Omi|Q11657596|King Achi}} in June of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}'', entry for June of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{jalink|Ten'ei|Q1193915}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{jalink|Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786}}" between {{jalink|Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624}}, retainer of {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{jalink|Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}{{jalink|Jinbohara Station|Q845133}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}{{jalink|Honjō Station|Q844906}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{jalink|Hachikō Line|Q1372071}}{{jalink|Tanshō Station|Q845044}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}

[[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}{{jalink|Kamisato|Q1345287}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{jalink|Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{jalink|Kamisato|Q1345287}}, {{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. Its deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} of {{jalink|Kami District|Q11634927}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}} says it descends from {{jalink|Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336}}, ancestor of {{jalink|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234}} ({{jalink|Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{jalink|Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949}}" who came from {{jalink|Daifang Commandery|Q704994}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{jalink|Achi no Omi|Q11657596|King Achi}} in June of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}'', entry for June of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{jalink|Ten'ei|Q1193915}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{jalink|Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786}}" between {{jalink|Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624}}, retainer of {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{jalink|Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}{{jalink|Jinbohara Station|Q845133}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}{{jalink|Honjō Station|Q844906}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{jalink|Hachikō Line|Q1372071}}{{jalink|Tanshō Station|Q845044}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}

It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} of {{jalink|Kami District|Q11634927}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}} says it descends from {{jalink|Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336}}, ancestor of {{jalink|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234}} ({{jalink|Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{jalink|Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949}}" who came from {{jalink|Daifang Commandery|Q704994}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{jalink|Achi no Omi|Q11657596|King Achi}} in June of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}'', entry for June of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{jalink|Ten'ei|Q1193915}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{jalink|Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786}}" between {{jalink|Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624}}, retainer of {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{jalink|Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}{{jalink|Jinbohara Station|Q845133}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}{{jalink|Honjō Station|Q844906}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{jalink|Hachikō Line|Q1372071}}{{jalink|Tanshō Station|Q845044}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}[[Category:qq]]

former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{jalink|Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{jalink|Kamisato|Q1345287}}, {{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. Its deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} of {{jalink|Kami District|Q11634927}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}} says it descends from {{jalink|Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336}}, ancestor of {{jalink|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234}} ({{jalink|Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{jalink|Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949}}" who came from {{jalink|Daifang Commandery|Q704994}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{jalink|Achi no Omi|Q11657596|King Achi}} in June of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}'', entry for June of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{jalink|Ten'ei|Q1193915}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{jalink|Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786}}" between {{jalink|Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624}}, retainer of {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{jalink|Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|WD=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|WD=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine".
