[[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|WD=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|WD=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* {'text': '埼玉県児玉郡上里町忍保', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagahatabe-jinja (Kamisato)}} [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|WD=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto|1=Takehazuchi}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927|1=Kami district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949|1=Seven-Surname Han People}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994|1=Daifang Commandery}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi|1=Achi no Omi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786|1=Battle of Kanagawa}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624|1=Takigawa Kazumasu}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948|1=Hōjō Ujimasa}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906|1=Honjō Station (Saitama)}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044|1=Tanshō Station}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|WD=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto|1=Takehazuchi}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927|1=Kami district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949|1=Seven-Surname Han People}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994|1=Daifang Commandery}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi|1=Achi no Omi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786|1=Battle of Kanagawa}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624|1=Takigawa Kazumasu}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948|1=Hōjō Ujimasa}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906|1=Honjō Station (Saitama)}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044|1=Tanshō Station}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927|1=Kami district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949|1=Seven-Surname Han People}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994|1=Daifang Commandery}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi|1=Achi no Omi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786|1=Battle of Kanagawa}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624|1=Takigawa Kazumasu}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948|1=Hōjō Ujimasa}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906|1=Honjō Station (Saitama)}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044|1=Tanshō Station}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|WD=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto|1=Takehazuchi}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927|1=Kami district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949|1=Seven-Surname Han People}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994|1=Daifang Commandery}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi|1=Achi no Omi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786|1=Battle of Kanagawa}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624|1=Takigawa Kazumasu}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948|1=Hōjō Ujimasa}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906|1=Honjō Station (Saitama)}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044|1=Tanshō Station}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはたへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)}}[[Category:qq]]

* {'text': '埼玉県児玉郡上里町忍保', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagahatabe-jinja (Kamisato)}} [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|WD=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto|1=Takehazuchi}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927|1=Kami district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949|1=Seven-Surname Han People}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994|1=Daifang Commandery}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi|1=Achi no Omi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786|1=Battle of Kanagawa}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624|1=Takigawa Kazumasu}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948|1=Hōjō Ujimasa}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906|1=Honjō Station (Saitama)}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044|1=Tanshō Station}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:qq]]

* {'text': '埼玉県児玉郡上里町忍保', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagahatabe-jinja (Kamisato)}} [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|WD=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto|1=Takehazuchi}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927|1=Kami district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949|1=Seven-Surname Han People}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994|1=Daifang Commandery}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi|1=Achi no Omi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786|1=Battle of Kanagawa}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624|1=Takigawa Kazumasu}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948|1=Hōjō Ujimasa}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906|1=Honjō Station (Saitama)}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044|1=Tanshō Station}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:qq]]

* {'text': '埼玉県児玉郡上里町忍保', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagahatabe-jinja (Kamisato)}} [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|WD=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto|1=Takehazuchi}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927|1=Kami district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949|1=Seven-Surname Han People}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994|1=Daifang Commandery}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi|1=Achi no Omi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786|1=Battle of Kanagawa}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624|1=Takigawa Kazumasu}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948|1=Hōjō Ujimasa}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906|1=Honjō Station (Saitama)}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044|1=Tanshō Station}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:qq]]

* {'text': '埼玉県児玉郡上里町忍保', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagahatabe-jinja (Kamisato)}} [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|WD=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto|1=Takehazuchi}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927|1=Kami district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949|1=Seven-Surname Han People}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994|1=Daifang Commandery}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi|1=Achi no Omi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786|1=Battle of Kanagawa}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624|1=Takigawa Kazumasu}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948|1=Hōjō Ujimasa}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906|1=Honjō Station (Saitama)}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044|1=Tanshō Station}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:qq]]

* {'text': '埼玉県児玉郡上里町忍保', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nagahatabe-jinja (Kamisato)}} [[JA:長幡部神社 (上里町)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Nagahatabe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Nagahama 1370 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Niu-sama |札所等 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=14|lat_sec=35.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=6|lon_sec=38.8|label=Nagahatabe Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|14|35.9|N|139|6|38.8|E}}}} }} '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (ながはたべじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone River system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Kami|Q10940642|WD=Q10940642|lt=Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto|1=Takehazuchi}}. It is identified with "Nagahatabe Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|WD=Q11634927|1=Kami district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Origin === The founding year is unknown. According to shrine tradition, its origin is that immigrant-descended clans with advanced weaving techniques enshrined the weaving deity "Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto" as guardian deity at Nishimatoba-nishi, Aza-Miya, on the south bank of the Kanna RiverCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Nagahatabe Shrine Origin''. "Nagahata" refers to a kind of silk fabric called "ashiginu," and the shrine name "Nagahatabe" indicates the artisan group "Nagahatabe clan" that wove it. Regarding the origin of that clan, the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} says it descends from {{ill|lt=Kami-Ōne-no-Ō|Q119380336|WD=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}}, ancestor of {{ill|lt=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11520234|WD=Q11520234|1=Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} ({{ill|lt=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11358204|WD=Q11358204|1=Minozaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}), while ''Asaue clan genealogy and estate record'' says Ame-no-Hatsuchio-no-Mikoto was the ancestral deity. Separate from the ancient native Nagahatabe clan, among the "{{ill|lt=Seven-Surname Han People|Q17211949|WD=Q17211949|1=Seven-Surname Han People}}" who came from {{ill|lt=Daifang Commandery|Q704994|WD=Q704994|1=Daifang Commandery}} on the Korean Peninsula with {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|lt=King Achi|1=Achi no Omi}} in June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785) to escape warfare, there was also a clan of the "Kō" surname''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', entry for June of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 (785)'''''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Vol. 23, lost text in entry on the Sakagami clan''' ::''In the reign of Emperor Ōjin, avoiding turmoil in the homeland, [they] came to Japan with mother, wife, children, younger brother Sensenkōtoku, and Han people of seven surnames...'' ::(full quotation omitted in translation context) . In {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 1 (1110), flooding of the Kanna River washed away the shrine grounds, so it was moved from Nishimatoba of Aza-Miya to present Ōaza Nagahama. In June of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10 (1582), Kami District of Musashi became the battlefield of the "{{ill|lt=Battle of Kanna River|Q1058786|WD=Q1058786|1=Battle of Kanagawa}}" between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|WD=Q953624|1=Takigawa Kazumasu}}, retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|WD=Q736948|1=Hōjō Ujimasa}}. War fire destroyed shrine buildings and old documents. In the Edo period, because the Niu deity was interpreted as earth, water, fire, metal, and wood deities, it was called "Goshonomiya"''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'', Nagahama-shimogō village. In Meiji 5, the shrine name was restored from "Nagahata Goshamiya" to the current "Nagahatabe Shrine". File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama).jpg|Haiden '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 4 (1915) March 12 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha === Precinct shrines === Merged before Meiji. * Kubo Inari ShrineCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Kōtaijinja Origin'' Merged in Meiji 41. "Kōtaijinja" was returned in Shōwa 21 (1946) to its former site in Kubo at strong local request. * Kubo Kōtaijinja (deity) Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto * Aza-Miya Niu-sha * Aza-Miya Inari-sha : subordinate shrine of Aza-Miya Niu-sha File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Honden.jpg|Honden File:Nagahatabe-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Nagahama) Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine === Events === * Spring festival (March 19) * Autumn festival (October 19) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus Komugicchi-go (south route) bound for "Unicus" from "Nishihara-chō" stop; get off at final stop "Minami Gomyō Kōkaidō," then 15 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Honjō Station|Q844906|WD=Q844906|1=Honjō Station (Saitama)}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Kansen Sōgō Shisho"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. * From JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}}{{ill|lt=Tanshō Station|Q845044|WD=Q845044|1=Tanshō Station}}, take Asahi Bus bound for "Honjō Station South Exit"; get off at "Sanchō," 30 min walk. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nagahatabe Shrine (Q72727981)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]]
